w20: Accelerator-optimized instance featuring dual NVIDIA A100 GPUs and 170GB RAM, designed for high-performance machine learning and large language model inference tasks.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g: Accelerator-optimized instance featuring 2 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 24 vCPUs, and 170GB RAM for intensive compute workloads.
c200: The Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server with 2 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 24 vCPUs, and 170GB RAM. It delivers high-tier performance for LLM inference and data science tasks.
w150: The Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized instance built on an x86_64 architecture. It utilizes an Intel Xeon processor with 24 vCPUs derived from 12 physical cores operating at 2.2 GHz, supported by 170 GB of memory. This configuration includes 2 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing a total of 80 GB of VRAM, while local storage is not bundled. Benchmark results indicate average multi-core CPU and memory performance, but the system excels in specialized tasks, achieving top-tier results for LLM inference across small, medium, and large models. Single-core performance metrics are categorized as weak. The instance supports AVX and AVX512F instruction sets, alongside cryptographic and virtualization security features. With no local storage and a focus on GPU-accelerated processing, this server is designed for demanding data science jobs, large language model inference, and complex computational workloads requiring significant parallel processing capabilities.
